missing_requirements.evidence_quote: The system shall transition from the Door Open with Item state to the Cooking state when the user presses the Start button.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall transition from the Door Open with Item state to the Cooking state when the user presses the Start button.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If instead the door were opened during cooking, the statechart would enter the Door Open with Item state
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall transition from the Cooking state to the Door Open with Item state if the door is opened during cooking.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall leave the Cooking state and reenter the Door Shut with Item state when the timer expires.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement was not mentioned in the source text. The correct behavior is that the statechart leaves the Cooking state and remains in the Cooking state until the timer expires, at which point it transitions back to the Door Shut with Item state.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall transition from the Door Shut state to the Door Open state when the user opens the door.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall transition from the Door Shut state to the Door Open state when the user opens the door.
duplicates.reason: This is a duplicate requirement. The source text only mentions this behavior once.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enter the Ready to Cook state after the user inputs the cooking time.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous because it does not specify what happens if the user inputs an invalid cooking time. The source text only mentions that the user inputs the cooking time, but it does not provide any additional context.

Source text:
<<<
As a second example of a statechart, consider a simplified version of the Microwave
Oven Control statechart, which is shown in Figure 10.5. The statechart shows the
different states for cooking food. The initial state is Door Shut. Consider a scenario
that starts when the user opens the door. As a result, the statechart transitions into
the Door Open state. The user then places an item in the oven, causing the statechart
to transition into the Door Open with Item state. When the user closes the door, the
statechart then transitions into the Door Shut with Item state. After the user inputs
the cooking time, the Ready to Cook state is entered. Next the user presses the Start
button, which causes the statechart to transition into the Cooking state. When the
timer expires, the statechart leaves the Cooking state and reenters the Door Shut with
Item state. If instead the door were opened during cooking, the statechart would
enter the Door Open with Item state
>>>

Rewritten requirements JSON:
{
  "rewritten_requirements": [
    "The system shall transition from the Door Shut state to the Door Open state when the user opens the door.",
    "The system shall transition from the Door Open state to the Door Open with Item state when the user places an item in the oven.",
    "The system shall transition from the Door Open with Item state to the Door Shut with Item state when the user closes the door.",
    "The system shall enter the Ready to Cook state after the user inputs the cooking time.",
    "The system shall transition from the Ready to Cook state to the Cooking state when the user presses the Start button.",
    "The system shall leave the Cooking state and reenter the Door Shut with Item state when the timer expires.",
    "The system shall transition from the Cooking state to the Door Open with Item state if the door is opened during cooking."
  ]
}
